Project: DAOS
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Open
Summary: daos_agent GetAttachInfo unsuccessful failures
Description: Conversation with Johann: We have quite a few nodes in our cluster that have two numa nodes but only one IB interface (the second NIC is not supported in 8.4). daos_agent net-scan correctly reports ib0 on numa node 1 as the only available interface. However, in order to run ior etc successfully. we have to add the below to our daos_agent.yml file, so that the agent tells client threads on both numa nodes to share the same interface. I would argue that since daos_agent knows there is only one interface, we shouldn't have to add this configuration to the yaml file in order to make daos work. Do you agree, should I open a ticket? fabric_ifaces: - numa_node: 0 devices: - iface: ib0 domain: mlx5_0 - numa_node: 1 devices: - iface: ib0 domain: mlx5_0 Johann asked for the failure symptoms. Here is some feedback from Sherin on how the actual errors appear: [root@hl-c500 ~]# lscpu | grep NUMA NUMA node(s): 2 NUMA node0 CPU(s): 0-7,16-23 NUMA node1 CPU(s): 8-15,24-31 [root@hl-c500 ~]# ibdev2netdev mlx5_0 port 1 ==> ib0 (Up) mlx5_1 port 1 ==> ib1 (Down) [root@hl-c500 ~]# daos_agent net-scan --------- localhost --------- ------------- NUMA Socket 1 ------------- Provider Interfaces -------- ---------- ofi+tcp ib0 ofi+sockets ib0 ofi+verbs;ofi_rxm ib0 ofi+tcp;ofi_rxm ib0 ofi+verbs ib0 [root@hl-c500 ~]# daos cont create --pool stg --type posix --label stgc mgmt ERR src/mgmt/cli_mgmt.c:272 get_attach_info() GetAttachInfo unsuccessful: 2 ERROR: daos: daos_init() failed: DER_MISC(-1025): Miscellaneous error [root@hl-c500 ~]# CRT_PHY_ADDR_STR="ofi+verbs;ofi_rxm" OFI_INTERFACE=ib0 OFI_DOMAIN=mlx5_0 daos cont create --pool stg --type posix --label stgc mgmt ERR src/mgmt/cli_mgmt.c:272 get_attach_info() GetAttachInfo unsuccessful: 2 ERROR: daos: daos_init() failed: DER_MISC(-1025): Miscellaneous error Johann asked for the following: root@hl-c500 ~]# lstopo-no-graphics | grep -B 4 mlx Block(Disk) "sdad" PCIBridge PCI 83:00.0 (InfiniBand) Net "ib0" OpenFabrics "mlx5_0" PCI 83:00.1 (InfiniBand) Net "ib1" OpenFabrics "mlx5_1" ib1 is the second port in the same card as ib0, there is no cable in that port. So maybe that second port is the real problem?

expected.summary: On nodes with two NUMA nodes but only one supported InfiniBand interface, daos_agent net-scan detects the available interface correctly, but client operations still fail with GetAttachInfo unsuccessful errors. The issue can be worked around by manually configuring both NUMA nodes to use the same interface in daos_agent.yml.
action_items: Investigate why GetAttachInfo fails when only one usable interface is present across two NUMA nodes.
Check whether daos_agent should automatically map both NUMA nodes to the detected interface in this setup.
Review the role of the second unsupported or unplugged port in triggering the failure.
risks: DAOS client operations can fail unless manual agent configuration is added.
Clusters with similar NUMA and NIC layouts may be misconfigured or unusable by default.
open_questions: Should daos_agent automatically share the single detected interface across both NUMA nodes?
Is the second port on the same card the cause of the GetAttachInfo failure?

input_text:
Project: WT
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Needs Scheduling
Summary: Timestamps do not work with logged tables.
Description: Recently some questions arose concerning the interaction between timestamps and logging and whether using both at once is a viable model for applications. Commits to logged tables are always immediately durable so the concept of rollback-to-stable and a stable timestamp does not apply, but it might still make sense to manage the oldest timestamp and maintain/read from history. However, this doesn't work. The major problem is that timestamp information is not written to the log. Thus, any value recovered from the log will lose its timestamp information, so if two successive values are committed and written to the log, and a crash happens before the next checkpoint, other history might remain but the first of the two will be lost. There is at least one additional problem, which is that rollback-to-stable will remove history store entries associated with logged tables if they are newer than the rollback timestamp; this would need to be changed to skip over the parts of the history store associated with logged tables. The conclusion seems to be that using timestamps and logging together is not viable and shouldn't be allowed. However, it needs to be possible to write to a logged table as part of a timestamped transaction (the oplog does this) so it isn't immediately clear what the best way to enforce this restriction is. The leading candidate so far is to drop timestamp information from updates to logged tables at the time they're created; this has a number of benefits (e.g. nothing special needs to be done on the read path, and nothing special needs to be done to avoid moving values to the history store) but might or might not turn out to actually be workable. The current documentation does not mention this combination (AFAIK) -- the questions arose as a consequence of wondering whether it should as part of other ongoing doc changes. The docs should eventually be updated once the way forward becomes clearer.
Comments:
1. [~haribabu.kommi], I'm happy to own making the changes, but would you please weigh in on this? Obviously, we'll need a careful rollout to MongoDB, but [~daniel.gottlieb]'s comments make me think there shouldn't be any problems there. cc: [~alexander.gorrod]
2. [~michael.cahill] I'd appreciate it if you can take a look at this as well. My preference is for the described solution as well - if timestamps aren't valid, getting rid of them as early as possible seems appealing. I'd also like to consider the possibility (albeit more complex) of implementing sub-transactions for this reason. That is an application could create a transaction, and then sub-transa
3. Another approach might be to use {{__wt_txn_op_set_timestamp()}} at commit time to clear logged updates to 0/MAX (and leaving the txn ID alone). But, this might result in records being written into the HS file during reconciliation, if not now, in the future?

expected.summary: Using timestamps together with logged tables is described as not viable because timestamp information is not written to the log and can be lost during recovery. The issue also affects rollback-to-stable behavior, and the way to enforce this restriction is still undecided.
action_items: Decide how to restrict or prevent use of timestamps with logged tables.
Evaluate dropping timestamp information from updates to logged tables at creation time.
Update rollback-to-stable handling so it skips history store entries associated with logged tables.
Update the documentation once the supported behavior is defined.
risks: Recovered logged values can lose timestamp information after a crash.
Rollback-to-stable can remove history store entries for logged tables.
Applications may encounter incorrect behavior if timestamps and logging are used together.
open_questions: How should the restriction be enforced while still allowing writes to logged tables inside timestamped transactions?
Should timestamp information be cleared at update creation time, at commit time, or through a more complex mechanism such as sub-transactions?

Project: XNIO
Issue Type: Feature Request
Status: Open
Summary: XNIO SelectorProvider loading via reflection is not compatible with JDK9 modules
Description: When running Undertow tests I've come across these warnings: {code} # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.516 WARNING: An illegal reflective access operation has occurred # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.516 WARNING: Illegal reflective access by org.xnio.nio.NioXnio$2 (file:/home/fla/.m2/repository/org/jboss/xnio/xnio-nio/3.3.8.Final/xnio-nio-3.3.8.Final.jar) to constructor sun.nio.ch.EPollSelectorProvider() # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.516 WARNING: Please consider reporting this to the maintainers of org.xnio.nio.NioXnio$2 # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.516 WARNING: Use --illegal-access=warn to enable warnings of further illegal reflective access operations # Created at 2020-11-24T07:46:18.517 WARNING: All illegal access operations will be denied in a future release {code} XNIO invokes the provider constructor via reflection. I think that we should invoke SelectorProvider.provider instead, unless there is a very good reason for not doing so. If there is a reason, the question that follows is if the reason applies only to JDK8, or if it applies to all JDKs. Keeping in mind that, a possible workaround is using the "java.nio.channels.spi.SelectorProvider" property to configure the specific provider according to OS, even if it has to be don in a trial and error way (ServiceConfigurationError will be thrown if the chosen provider is not available).

expected.summary: XNIO loads SelectorProvider via reflection, which produces illegal reflective access warnings and is not compatible with JDK 9 modules. The request suggests using SelectorProvider.provider() instead, or using the java.nio.channels.spi.SelectorProvider property as a workaround if needed.
action_items: Investigate replacing reflective SelectorProvider construction with SelectorProvider.provider().
Determine whether reflection is still required on JDK 8 or on all supported JDK versions.
Evaluate using the java.nio.channels.spi.SelectorProvider property as a fallback workaround.
risks: Future JDK releases may deny illegal reflective access and break this code path.
Continuing to use reflection reduces compatibility with JDK module rules.
open_questions: Why is SelectorProvider currently instantiated through reflection instead of SelectorProvider.provider()?
If reflection is required, does that requirement apply only to JDK 8 or to all supported JDKs?

expected.summary: AS AMENDED a bill for an ordinance amending the Denver Zoning Code to revise parking exemptions for pre-existing small zone lots. Approves a text amendment to the Denver Zoning Code to revise the Pre-Existing Small Zone Lot parking exemption. The Committee approved filing this bill at its meeting on 2-14-17. On 2-27-17, Council held this item in Committee to 3-20-17. Amended 3-20-17 to ensure that the parking exemption is applied for all uses. Some parking requirements are calculated based on gross floor area while others are on number of units and not explicitly for gross floor area, to further clarify the legislative intent of the proposed bill to emphasize the city’s commitment to more comprehensively address transportation demand management strategies in the short term, and to require a Zoning Permit with Informational Notice for all new buildings on Pre-Existing Small Zone Lots that request to use the small lot parking exemption; Enables all expansions to existing buildings to receive the full parking exemption; and clarifies at what point an “existing building” is considered

Description: Hi, I'm using LDAP to a Windows Active Directory as the authentication method for SpringXD. But after the user is authenticated in the SpringXD, the user is not authorized to do anythin. Based on the guides and the configuration files, it is unclear where the authorization rules for each user should be put, ROLE_VIEW, ROLE_CREATE, ROLE_ADMIN. Can the SpringXD team guide me to where will the authorization rules be created? In SpringXD servers.yml file or in Active Directory? Regards, Yew Wee

My suspicion is because the SpringXD search based on "uid=User1,OU=Users,OU=Company, DN=COMPANY, D
3. input_text:
Subject: Re:

Traveling to have a business meeting takes the fun out of the trip.  Especially if you have to prepare a presentation.  I would suggest holding the business plan meetings here then take a trip without any formal business meetings.  I would even try and get some honest opinions on whether a trip is even desired or necessary.

As far as the business meetings, I think it would be more productive to try and stimulate discussions across the different groups about what is working and what is not.  Too often the presenter speaks and the others are quiet just waiting for their turn.   The meetings might be better if held in a round table discussion format.  

My suggestion for where to go is Austin.  Play golf and rent a ski boat and jet ski's.  Flying somewhere takes too much time.
